questions.text: My mother offered me milk. But for my life, I could not drink it.[The underlined expression means:]
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: It is a phrase used for negative expressions specifying that no matter how hard I try, I can’t do it or can’t understand even if my life depends on it.
It indicates that by drinking milk he’ll put his life in danger but if this was the case, the mother won’t even offer the milk.

options.text: For the sake of my life

options.explanation: The phrase doesn’t correspond with the above-mentioned statement.

questions.explanation: My mother offered me milk. But for my life, I could not drink it. [The underlined expression means:] However hard I may try
